vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1052: Activity feed: per-turn observations with time-bucketed sidebar

plan.title: Activity feed: per-turn observations with time-bucketed sidebar
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: The dashboard shows agent state (running, blocked, in review) but not agent progress. Operators cannot triage 8 parallel workspaces without attaching to tmux. Agents have no structured memory across compaction or sessions — context dies with each conversation. There is no retrieval substrate for context injection, no audit of what each agent decided, and no cross-session continuity.
narratives.Proposal: Add a memory subsystem that captures per-turn LLM-extracted observations from agent transcripts, rolls them into per-workspace status, and surfaces them as a time-bucketed activity sidebar in the dashboard. Architecture: hybrid trigger model (Claude Code Stop hook + central transcript poller fed by a harness-adapter interface) sharing a single atomic-checkpoint-claim extraction pipeline; observation rows are FTS5-indexed in a project-scoped SQLite DB with porter+unicode61 tokenizer and a recency-decay + tag-boost re-ranker; injection runs at agent spawn time via Mustache MEMORY_CONTEXT and on every user prompt via UserPromptSubmit hook with query expansion. Identity is explicit (projectId, workspaceId, issueId, runId, sessionId, agentRole, agentHarness) — git branch is metadata only. Storage is central at ~/.panopticon/memory/<projectId>/<issueId>/ so deep-wipe never destroys audit. Extraction LLM is configurable via a provider registry (Haiku 4.5 default, cliproxy/gpt-4.1-nano alternate, env-var override, per-day cost cap, fallback chain). Cross-project search and pinned docs deferred to PAN-1116 and PAN-1117.

items.title: Define memory contracts in @panctl/contracts (Observation, Status, PendingTurn, ResetMarker, RagDecision)

narrative.Action: Add packages/contracts/src/memory.ts exporting Schema definitions and TypeScript types for Observation, Status, PendingTurn, ResetMarker, RagDecision, ExtractionProviderConfig. Every observation carries explicit identity (projectId, workspaceId, issueId, runId, sessionId, agentRole, agentHarness, gitBranch, sourceTranscriptOffset). Re-export from packages/contracts/src/index.ts. This is the contract that every downstream bead reads — get it wrong and 30+ beads rebuild.

subItems.title: Observation Schema includes id, timestamp, identity fields, actionStatus|null, narrative, summary, files[], tags[], tokens, model, sourceTranscriptOffset
Status Schema includes name, headline, summary, goal|null, phase enum, accomplished[], decided[], open[], nextSteps[], confidence, workingSet[], tags[]
PendingTurn Schema and ResetMarker Schema and RagDecision Schema all defined and exported
ExtractionProviderConfig Schema defines provider, model, env-var override, per-day cost cap, fallback chain
git branch is a metadata field only — no Schema permits using it as identity for lookup or filtering
packages/contracts build passes; types exported from index.ts; no circular imports

items.title: Add memory domain events to @panctl/contracts events union

narrative.Action: Add MemoryObservationCreatedEvent, MemoryStatusUpdatedEvent, MemoryRollupTriggeredEvent, MemoryResetMarkerCreatedEvent, MemoryHealthChangedEvent to packages/contracts/src/events.ts and include them in the discriminated union consumed by event-store, read-model, RPC, and the Zustand reducer.

subItems.title: Five new event types defined with type, sequence, timestamp, payload shape
Discriminated union exported and accepted by applyEventReducer
Effect/Schema validation round-trips all five event payloads in a unit test

items.title: Storage path resolution module (src/lib/memory/paths.ts)

narrative.Action: Export pure functions: resolveMemoryRoot(projectId), resolveIssueMemoryRoot(projectId, issueId), resolveObservationsFile(projectId, issueId, date), resolvePendingDir, resolveStatusFile, resolveArchiveDir, resolveSummariesDir, resolveRagRunsFile, resolveCheckpointFile(workspacePath), resolveFtsDbPath(projectId). All paths under ~/.panopticon/memory/<projectId>/ except the checkpoint, which lives in the worktree's .pan/memory-checkpoint.json.

subItems.title: All path functions return absolute paths using existing PANOPTICON_HOME from src/lib/paths.ts
Functions do not create directories; ensureDir helpers are separate and idempotent
Unit tests cover all path-construction permutations

items.title: SQLite FTS5 schema and reset_markers table (project-scoped DB)

narrative.Action: Create migration creating memory_fts virtual table (porter unicode61) with columns: content, display_content UNINDEXED, source, branch, entry_date, entry_time, entry_type, files, tags UNINDEXED, doc_type UNINDEXED, scope UNINDEXED, project_id, workspace_id, issue_id, run_id, session_id, agent_role, agent_harness. Also create reset_markers (scope, scope_id, from_timestamp, reason, created_at) and observation_index (id, observation_path_jsonl, byte_offset) for back-references. DB lives at ~/.panopticon/memory/<projectId>/memory-search.db, opened lazily per project.

subItems.title: Migration creates memory_fts with porter+unicode61 tokenizer and the documented columns
reset_markers and observation_index tables created with appropriate indexes
DB connection helper opens per-project DB lazily, caches handles, closes on dashboard shutdown
Async-only DB API — no readFileSync/writeFileSync/execSync in any code path reachable from dashboard routes
Migration idempotent on re-run

items.title: transcript_checkpoints table and atomic claimRange()

narrative.Action: Add transcript_checkpoints (session_id PK, project_id, workspace_id, issue_id, transcript_path, last_offset, last_observation_at, last_mid_turn_at, mid_turn_count_in_current_turn). Implement claimRange(sessionId, expectedFromOffset, toOffset): atomically advances last_offset and returns the claimed byte range or empty if a concurrent caller already advanced past expectedFromOffset. Use SQLite UPDATE ... WHERE last_offset = expectedFromOffset RETURNING. This is the race-safety lynchpin between hook and poller.

subItems.title: claimRange is atomic — concurrent callers with overlapping ranges produce exactly one winner; loser sees empty range
Concurrency test fires 50 simultaneous claimRange calls against the same session and asserts no duplicate ranges, no gaps
Mid-turn rate-limit fields (last_mid_turn_at, mid_turn_count_in_current_turn) included; reset on each Stop-hook claim
No long-lived locks held across LLM calls

items.title: Turn compression: JSONL delta -> compact U/A/Created/Updated text

narrative.Action:
src/lib/memory/compress.ts: parse a byte range of a Claude Code JSONL transcript into the compressed format `U: <user>
A: <assistant>
Created: f1, f2
Updated: f3
Subagents: ...
MCPs: ...
Skills: ...`. Categorize tool calls (Write -> Created, Edit -> Updated). Truncate user content to 40,000 chars. Handle partial trailing lines via offset bookkeeping.

subItems.title: Reads only the [fromOffset, toOffset] byte range; never the whole file
Returns a structured result {text, eventsConsumed, lastFullLineOffset}
Trailing partial JSONL line is preserved for the next call (returned lastFullLineOffset)
Tool-call categorization covers Write/Edit/MultiEdit/NotebookEdit/Bash; unknown tools annotated
User message > 40,000 chars is truncated with a marker

items.title: Extraction LLM provider registry (Anthropic SDK + cliproxy adapters)

narrative.Action: src/lib/memory/providers/{index.ts, anthropic.ts, cliproxy.ts, types.ts}. Provider interface: extract(prompt, schema, options) -> {data, usage, cost, model}. Anthropic adapter uses @anthropic-ai/sdk with prompt caching. cliproxy adapter posts to local cliproxy /v1/messages. Registry resolves the active provider from Settings or env-var override. Cost is computed per-call from token usage.

subItems.title: ExtractionProvider interface defined with extract(prompt, jsonSchema, options) returning typed response + usage + cost
Anthropic adapter uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 by default; supports prompt caching; honors model override
Cliproxy adapter routes through local sidecar at default cliproxy port; supports gpt-4.1-nano model option
Env-var override (PANOPTICON_MEMORY_PROVIDER, PANOPTICON_MEMORY_MODEL) takes precedence over Settings
Conformance test: a fake stub provider satisfies the interface and round-trips through the registry
Cost recorded per call via existing cost-events-db.ts

items.title: Per-day cost cap enforcement and provider fallback chain

narrative.Action: Before each provider call, check today's accumulated memory-extraction spend against the configured per-day cap (default $5/project/day). If at cap, skip extraction and log to health.json. On provider error or timeout, try the configured fallback provider once before dropping. Never block the agent.

subItems.title: Per-day spend computed from cost-events-db rows tagged source='memory-extraction'
When cap reached, extraction returns null with reason='cost-cap'; health.json updated; no agent-visible error
Provider error triggers single fallback attempt; double-failure drops the observation and records reason='extraction-failed'

items.title: Observation extractor: prompt assembly + JSON-schema response

narrative.Action: src/lib/memory/extract.ts: assemble the extraction prompt (compressed turn + last 3 observations + branch + agent role) and call the registered provider with a JSON schema demanding {narrative, summary, actionStatus, tags, files}. Returns null actionStatus for non-concrete turns (pure discussion). Validates response against Schema before returning. Domain tags (review-blocker, test-failure, merge-risk, user-preference, architecture-decision, worktree-state, handoff, regression) are listed in the prompt as preferred tag values when applicable.

subItems.title: Prompt instructs lead-with-outcomes framing and explicit null-actionStatus rule for discussion turns
Last 3 observations included as continuity/dedup context
Response validated against Observation Schema; malformed responses retry once then drop
Prompt declares the Panopticon domain tags as preferred values when applicable
Unit test with fixture compressed turn produces a valid observation against the stub provider

items.title: extractFromTranscriptDelta() orchestrator (single source of truth)

narrative.Action: src/lib/memory/pipeline.ts: extractFromTranscriptDelta({sessionId, transcriptPath, fromOffset, toOffset, identity, trigger}) chains claimRange -> compress -> extract -> writeObservation -> writePending -> indexFts -> emitDomainEvent -> maybeTriggerRollup. Pure async, awaits nothing harness-blocking, handles all failure modes by logging to health.json and returning a result.

subItems.title: All seven stages wired in order; each stage is independently testable
Pipeline never throws; returns {status, observation|null, reason}
If claimRange returns empty, pipeline noops immediately and returns {status:'noop'}
Subagent identity (agent_id present) -> early return with reason='subagent'

items.title: Observation writer: append JSONL + render markdown mirror

narrative.Action: Append observation JSON to observations/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl (atomic via O_APPEND), render a human-readable bullet in observations/YYYY-MM-DD.md (atomic write-temp + rename). All async (fs/promises).

subItems.title: JSONL append is atomic and survives concurrent writers
Markdown mirror updated in place; rerun does not produce duplicates
All file IO is async; no sync calls in code reachable from server routes

items.title: FTS indexer: insert observation row into project memory_fts

narrative.Action: On every observation write, insert a row into the per-project memory_fts table with content=narrative+summary, identity fields, tags, files, doc_type='observation', scope='workspace'. Also write a back-reference row into observation_index pointing at the source JSONL file + byte offset.

subItems.title: Insert wired into the pipeline writeObservation step
Insert failure does not block JSONL write; logged to health.json with reason='fts-index-failed'
observation_index back-reference round-trips: id -> jsonl path + byte_offset -> original JSON

items.title: Worker pool for extraction; health.json telemetry; never block agent

narrative.Action: Single dashboard-side worker queue processes pipeline jobs. Maintains health.json with last_success, last_failure, counts by reason. Bounded concurrency (default 4 concurrent extractions across all sessions). On crash, queue is replayed via reconciliation sweep.

subItems.title: Worker queue with bounded concurrency, configurable in Settings
health.json tracks extractions_attempted, succeeded, failed_by_reason with timestamps
MemoryHealthChangedEvent emitted on transitions between healthy/degraded/failing states

items.title: POST /api/memory/turn — Stop-hook receiver

narrative.Action: Effect route handler that accepts hook input {session_id, transcript_path, stop_hook_active, agent_id?, identity?}, validates, checks subagent filter, enqueues a pipeline job, returns 202 in <100ms. No sync FS calls. The hook handler does NOT await the LLM call.

subItems.title: POST /api/memory/turn validates Schema and returns 202 within 100ms in normal path (verified by latency test)
Returns 204 (no-op) when agent_id present (subagent)
Returns 422 if identity (projectId/issueId/workspaceId) cannot be resolved

items.title: Install Stop / SessionStart / UserPromptSubmit hooks at workspace setup

narrative.Action: Extend workspace-start logic (caveman/workspace.ts) to inject memory hooks into worktree .claude/settings.json: Stop hook posts to /api/memory/turn, SessionStart hook registers the session with the central poller and records identity, UserPromptSubmit hook posts to /api/memory/inject. Idempotent — re-running workspace setup does not duplicate hook entries.

subItems.title: Three hook entries installed at workspace setup, idempotently
Hook scripts emit hook input as JSON to the dashboard endpoint and return immediately
Regression test: deep-wipe workspace, recreate, hooks still present

items.title: Subagent filter at hook + poller (agent_id presence)

narrative.Action: When hook input contains agent_id, hook receiver returns 204 and pipeline early-exits. Poller registry never registers subagent sessions. Spawn a subagent and verify no observation row is created.

subItems.title: Hook receiver short-circuits on agent_id presence
Poller registry excludes subagent sessionIds
Integration test: spawn a Claude Code subagent (Explore type), verify no observation row was written

items.title: Central transcript poller (registry-driven, mtime fast-path, rate-limited)

narrative.Action: src/lib/memory/poller.ts: single setInterval(2000) walking an in-memory registry of {sessionId, transcriptPath, identity, harness, lastSize, lastMtime}. For each entry: fs.stat first; if unchanged, skip. If grown, tail-read [lastOffset, currentSize], count newlines, fire pipeline only if activity threshold (20 lines since last extraction) AND mid-turn rate limit (max 1/60s, max 3/turn) allow. Registry is lifecycle-gated: only running/working agents in hybrid or poll mode are registered.

subItems.title: Single setInterval(2000); empty registry means no syscalls, no reads, no LLM calls
fs.stat short-circuit prevents reads on idle transcripts
Mid-turn rate limit enforced: max 1 extraction/60s/session, max 3/turn (turn boundary marked by Stop-hook claim)
Registry registration tied to agent lifecycle state; subagents never registered; completed/dead agents removed
Concurrent fire with Stop hook: claimRange ensures exactly one wins; loser noops

items.title: TranscriptSource harness adapter (Claude Code; pi stub)

narrative.Action: Interface TranscriptSource { getActiveTranscripts(): Promise<Entry[]>, parseDelta(buffer): TurnEvent[] }. Claude Code adapter resolves transcript path from session metadata. Pi adapter ships as a stub returning [] (no-op) so the poller compiles for future harness work.

subItems.title: TranscriptSource interface defined; Claude Code adapter implemented
Pi adapter stub present; documented as 'wire when pi transcript surface is defined'
Poller consumes adapters via registry; adding a new harness requires only a new adapter, no poller changes

items.title: Reconciliation sweep on dashboard start and heartbeat-death

narrative.Action: On dashboard startup, walk all sessions with checkpoints whose lifecycle is stale, replay unprocessed deltas via the pipeline. Wire into existing heartbeat-death detection so dead sessions get a final extraction pass before the registry removes them. Idempotent via claimRange.

subItems.title: Dashboard startup hook invokes the sweep; sweep is bounded and logs progress
Heartbeat-death event subscriber fires per-session reconciliation
Idempotency test: running the sweep twice produces no duplicate observations

items.title: Pending turn writer (<millis>_<sessionId>.json)

narrative.Action: Write each compressed turn payload to pending/<millis>_<sessionId>.json. Used by status rollup to synthesize the next status update. Distinct from observations.

subItems.title: Pending file written atomically alongside observation
Filename embeds millis + sessionId for chronological listing

items.title: Status rollup trigger: fire when pending count >= 4 (workspace-wide)

narrative.Action: After each pending write, count pending/*.json. If >= 4 (configurable in Settings), enqueue a status rollup job. Workspace-wide count, not per-session.

subItems.title: Threshold reads from Settings with default 4; updateable without restart
Count includes pending files from all sessions in the workspace
Multiple concurrent triggers collapse to a single in-flight rollup per workspace

items.title: Status rollup LLM: synthesize workspace status from observations + pending

narrative.Action: src/lib/memory/rollup.ts: call provider with last 20 observations + last 3 archived statuses + new pending turns. Output the full Status Schema (name, headline, summary, goal, phase, accomplished, decided, open, nextSteps, confidence, workingSet, tags). Status is NOT cumulative — each call can ADD/UPDATE/DELETE/NOOP fields relative to previous status.

subItems.title: Prompt includes last 3 archived statuses + last 20 observations + new pending turns
Response validated against Status Schema; malformed responses retry once then drop
workingSet is refreshed each cycle, NOT cumulative
phase is one of exploring|planning|building|verifying|cleaning|shipping

items.title: Idempotent rollup commit: archive previous, write new, then clear pending

narrative.Action: Rollup commit sequence: (1) archive previous status to archive/YYYY-MM-DD_<slug>.json, (2) write new status.json atomically, (3) emit MemoryStatusUpdatedEvent, (4) clear pending/ entries that were included in this rollup. Any failure before step 4 leaves pending intact so the next observation re-fires the rollup. Keep last 3 archived statuses; older are pruned.

subItems.title: Failure between step 1 and step 4 leaves pending intact (re-rollup is safe)
archive/ retains last 3 statuses; older statuses pruned
MemoryStatusUpdatedEvent emitted only after status.json commits

items.title: FTS query with porter tokenizer + overfetch

narrative.Action: src/lib/memory/search.ts: searchMemory({query, projectId, workspaceId?, issueId?, limit, tags?}) -> hits[]. Builds MATCH query, overfetches limit*3 from FTS, returns rows with raw BM25 + metadata for the re-ranker.

subItems.title: MATCH query escapes user input correctly
Overfetch ratio = 3x of requested limit
Reset markers applied as WHERE timestamp > <latest marker> for the scope

items.title: Re-ranker: recency decay + tag boost + high-signal exemption

narrative.Action: Apply score = bm25 * e^(-0.02 * ageDays) + 0.3 * matchingTagCount. High-signal tags (error, decision, blocker, shipped, architecture, review-blocker, test-failure, merge-risk, architecture-decision, regression) get score floor of 1.0 if <72h. Truncate to requested limit after re-rank.

subItems.title: Re-rank applied after overfetch; final result count matches requested limit
High-signal tags within 72h escape decay (verified in unit test)
Tag boost: +0.3 per query term matching a tag

items.title: Query expansion (cheap LLM call, session-cached)

narrative.Action: Before retrieval, expand the user's prompt + last 3 observations into 3-5 BM25 search terms via the configured provider. Cache by (sessionId, contentHash) for the session lifetime. Skip + log if expansion fails.

subItems.title: Cache lookup by (sessionId, contentHash) skips the LLM call on cache hit
Expansion failure does not block retrieval; falls back to using raw prompt as the BM25 query
Expansion decision logged to rag-runs

items.title: Same-project sibling search predicate

narrative.Action: Search variant: sibling mode applies projectId = currentProjectId AND workspaceId != currentWorkspaceId AND issueId != currentIssueId. Identity-based — NEVER uses git branch. Token budget for sibling hits is separate (default 1500 tokens).

subItems.title: Predicate uses workspaceId/issueId, never git branch
Current workspace cannot leak into sibling results (regression test against the reference impl bug)
Sibling hits labeled with workspace/issue provenance in returned results

items.title: Reset markers: filter retrieval at read time without deleting data

narrative.Action: reset_markers (scope, scope_id, from_timestamp, reason, created_at). Every retrieval applies AND timestamp > <latest applicable marker>. Auto-marker created on post-merge lifecycle (clears review-blocker noise). Manual via pan memory reset. CLI override --include-archived bypasses the filter.

subItems.title: Markers applied to all retrieval paths (sibling, prompt-time, spawn-time)
Post-merge lifecycle creates a workspace-scoped marker automatically
JSONL files are never modified by reset operations
MemoryResetMarkerCreatedEvent emitted on marker creation

items.title: Spawn-time MEMORY_CONTEXT injection in role prompt templates

narrative.Action: Before each agent spawn (plan, work, review, test, ship), retrieve relevant observations + current status + sibling hints, render into a MEMORY_CONTEXT Mustache variable, inject into the role prompt. Budgets: status 2000, observations 5000, summaries 500, sibling 1500 tokens. Log decisions to rag-runs.

subItems.title: MEMORY_CONTEXT variable available in all five role templates
Token budgets enforced per category (status/observations/summaries/sibling)
If no relevant memory exists, MEMORY_CONTEXT renders as empty (not 'no context found' boilerplate)
Sibling hits rendered as hints with workspace/issue provenance, NOT as authoritative current state
Each spawn-time retrieval decision logged to rag-runs with outcome (injected, skipped, no-hits, budget-truncated)

items.title: UserPromptSubmit prompt-time injection (receiver + budgets + telemetry)

narrative.Action: POST /api/memory/inject accepts user prompt + sessionId, runs query expansion, searches with same-project + sibling, applies budgets, returns context to inject. UserPromptSubmit hook invokes this and feeds the result back into the agent's context. Every decision (skipped, no-hits, expansion-failed, context-too-large, injected) logged to rag-runs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl.

subItems.title: POST /api/memory/inject returns context within 1s p95 (verified by latency test)
Token budgets enforced (status 2k, observations 5k, summaries 500, sibling 1.5k)
rag-runs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl records the decision with reasons, expansion result, hit counts, byte allocations
Settings toggle disables prompt-time injection without affecting spawn-time injection
Failure modes (expansion failed, no hits, budget exceeded) never block the agent; rag-runs entry written instead

items.title: Daily summary generator (CLI + dashboard button)

narrative.Action: Generate summaries/YYYY-MM-DD.md from the day's observations. Minimum 3 observations required. Regenerate allowed if 20+ new observations since last summary. Promote summaries into FTS as compact replacements for old observations after 30 days.

subItems.title: Minimum 3 observations enforced; below threshold returns 'insufficient data'
Regeneration only if >= 20 new observations since last summary
Summary indexed into FTS with doc_type='summary'

items.title: Wire memory events through Effect RPC subscribeDomainEvents

narrative.Action: Read-model handles five new event types via shared applyEventReducer. RPC client picks them up automatically through existing subscription. No new endpoints. Snapshot includes memory state for currently-active workspaces.

subItems.title: Read-model handlers added for all five memory events
Snapshot getSnapshot() returns memory state alongside existing state
Events flow end-to-end: pipeline emit -> event store -> RPC stream -> Zustand store

items.title: Zustand store slice for observations + status + reset markers

narrative.Action: Add slices to useDashboardStore: observationsByIssueId (rolling buffer of recent obs per issue), statusByIssueId, resetMarkersByScope. Reducers shared from @panctl/contracts. Rolling buffer cap (default 50 per issue) to keep memory bounded.

subItems.title: Three slices added with typed reducers
Rolling buffer caps at 50 observations per issue (configurable)
Reducers exported from @panctl/contracts and used by both server read-model and frontend store

items.title: Time-bucket helper (Just Now / Earlier Today / Yesterday / This Week / This Month / Older)

narrative.Action: Pure function bucketByTime(items, nowTs?) -> {justNow:[], earlierToday:[], yesterday:[], thisWeek:[], thisMonth:[], older:[]}. Uses local timezone. Empty buckets omitted in caller's render.

subItems.title: Six bucket categories returned with documented boundaries
Local timezone honored (test with TZ=America/Los_Angeles and TZ=UTC fixtures)
Boundary cases tested: exactly 1h, midnight, exactly 7 days, last-of-month

items.title: ActivityFeedSidebar component (bucketed actionStatus list)

narrative.Action: Sidebar component selecting observations from store, filtering actionStatus !== null, bucketing via h3, rendering each bucket as a header + list. Memoized selectors to avoid cascading re-renders. Empty buckets are skipped.

subItems.title: Renders only observations with non-null actionStatus
Empty buckets do not render their headers
Memoized selectors verified via React DevTools profile (no unrelated re-renders)
Each item shows actionStatus + workspace/issue label + relative time

items.title: WorkspaceStatusCard component

narrative.Action: Card rendering: phase icon (colored by phase enum), headline, summary line-clamped to 3 lines, last 3 observations with actionStatus as bullets, stats footer (additions/deletions/commits/PRs). Click navigates to workspace home tab.

subItems.title: Phase icon colored by phase enum (exploring/planning/building/verifying/cleaning/shipping)
Summary line-clamped to 3 lines with ellipsis overflow
Up to 3 recent non-null-actionStatus observations rendered as bullets
Click navigates to workspace home tab (not a specific conversation)
Stale-status indicator shown if last observation > 1h old

items.title: Real-time sidebar updates via existing RPC event stream

narrative.Action: MemoryObservationCreatedEvent flowing through existing subscribeDomainEvents updates the Zustand store; sidebar re-renders with the new entry in Just Now. No polling. For the global home page, a 2s/3min fallback poller covers the initial-load window where the WebSocket may not yet be connected.

subItems.title: New observation event triggers UI update within 500ms in normal path
Fallback poll runs only for 3 minutes after page load, then disables itself

items.title: Settings UI: memory provider + cost cap + feature toggles + rollup threshold

narrative.Action: New Memory section in dashboard Settings page: provider picker (Anthropic / cliproxy), model picker, env-var override hint, per-day cost cap, fallback provider, feature toggles (disable observations, disable prompt-time injection), rollup threshold (default 4), sidebar refresh interval. Persisted via existing Settings storage.

subItems.title: All settings persisted and round-trip through the Settings storage layer
Toggling 'disable observations' stops hook/poller registration without restart
Env-var override hint clearly documents PANOPTICON_MEMORY_PROVIDER / PANOPTICON_MEMORY_MODEL precedence over UI
Cost cap field validates numeric input; 0 disables the cap

items.title: pan memory CLI umbrella (search / status / reset / summary / doctor)

narrative.Action: Add pan memory verb with subcommands: search (with --project --workspace --issue --tag --sibling), status, reset (creates a reset marker), summary (generates daily summary), doctor (prints health.json + last extraction times + pending counts + provider config). Wrapper SKILL.md at skills/pan-memory/SKILL.md. Lint passes per scripts/lint-skills.sh.

subItems.title: All five subcommands implemented and wired into the pan dispatcher
skills/pan-memory/SKILL.md created; npm run lint passes the skills lint
pan memory search supports --sibling for same-project sibling mode
pan memory doctor exits non-zero if last successful extraction > 1h ago for any active agent

items.title: End-to-end test: spawn agent, exercise multi-turn flow, verify pipeline + UI

narrative.Action: Vitest + Playwright. Spawn a real Claude Code agent with a stub provider, run 6 simulated turns, assert: 6 observations exist with correct identity, status rollup fired at turn 4, sidebar shows 'Just Now' bucket with 3 most recent items, retrieval returns expected hits, reset marker filters older observations, subagent spawn produces no observation, cost recorded in cost-events-db.

subItems.title: 6 observations written with correct projectId/workspaceId/issueId/sessionId/agentRole
Status rollup fired exactly once at the 4-turn threshold
Sidebar renders 3 most recent non-null actionStatus items under Just Now
Reset marker excludes pre-marker observations from retrieval; --include-archived restores them
Subagent spawn produces no durable observation row
Cost recorded in cost-events-db with source='memory-extraction'
Concurrent hook+poller fire on same delta produces exactly one observation
